description.value: Microsoft's proprietary enterprise relational database management system for transactional and analytical workloads.
description.comment: Enterprise RDBMS supporting on-premises (Windows/Linux) and cloud (Azure SQL).

website.comment: Official product page; derived from Microsoft's SQL Server site.

license.comment: Closed-source commercial product (paid editions plus free Express/Developer editions); not open source.

first-release.comment: SQL Server 1.0 (for OS/2) released in 1989; the Microsoft-developed rewrite (SQL Server 4.21 / 6.0 for Windows NT) followed in 1993-1995.

latest-version.comment: SQL Server 2025 reached general availability on 2025-11-18; internal version 17.x. Checked 2026-06-09.

actively-maintained.comment: New major release (2025) GA in Nov 2025 with ongoing cumulative updates.

database-type.comment: Primarily a relational RDBMS; also supports JSON, graph, and vector data, but relational is the core model.

acid-compliant.comment: Full ACID transaction support with configurable isolation levels by default.

schema-model.comment: Schema-enforced relational model; defined table schemas are required.

horizontal-scaling.comment: No native horizontal data sharding built in; scale-out is via read replicas/Always On AGs or external partitioning, not automatic sharding. Per RESEARCH.md, horizontal scaling must be a built-in feature.

replication.comment: Always On availability groups, log shipping, and transactional/merge/snapshot/peer-to-peer replication. Peer-to-peer offers limited multi-primary, but the primary supported model is primary-replica.

clustering.comment: Built-in Always On Failover Cluster Instances and availability groups (requires Windows Server Failover Cluster / cluster manager on the OS side).

cloud-offering.comment: Official Microsoft managed services: Azure SQL Database and Azure SQL Managed Instance, plus SQL Server on Azure VMs.

full-text-search.comment: Built-in Full-Text Search component supporting CONTAINS/FREETEXT queries.

json-support.comment: SQL Server 2025 adds a native binary JSON data type with built-in functions; earlier versions stored JSON as text with queryable functions.

geospatial.comment: Native geometry and geography spatial data types with spatial indexes and functions.

time-series.comment: No specialized time-series engine; temporal (system-versioned) tables exist but are not dedicated time-series storage.

stored-procedures.comment: T-SQL and CLR stored procedures and functions supported.

triggers.comment: DML, DDL, and logon triggers supported.

official-gui.comment: SQL Server Management Studio (SSMS) and the cross-platform Azure Data Studio / mssql extension are official GUI tools.

cli-tool.comment: Official sqlcmd utility (and bcp) command-line tools.

docker-image.comment: Microsoft publishes official images as a Verified Publisher on Docker Hub (microsoft/mssql-server) and via mcr.microsoft.com/mssql/server.

primary-language.comment: Core database engine implemented primarily in C++ (with C); not officially published as a precise breakdown, but widely documented.

github-stars.comment: Closed-source; no primary public GitHub source repository. Per RESEARCH.md, use null for closed-source databases.

stackoverflow-tags.comment: Stack Overflow tag pages could not be fetched in this environment to obtain a verifiable count; left null rather than cite an unverified number. The 'sql-server' tag has on the order of 300k+ questions historically.

db-engines-rank.comment: Ranked #3 in the DB-Engines ranking (June 2026, score 698.04), behind Oracle and MySQL. Time-sensitive; checked 2026-06-09.

learning-curve.comment: Standard SQL (T-SQL) with excellent documentation and mature tooling (moderate), but enterprise features (Always On, editions, licensing, administration) add conceptual complexity; rated middle of the 1-5 scale.
